text: Fill in the blanks on oncogenesis, tumour markers, HIV, alcohol metabolism, and AI in biochemistry.
questions: When a proto-oncogene is mutated, it becomes an *oncogene/Oncogene* — a permanently active growth signal. The *RAS/Ras* oncogene is mutated in approximately *30/thirty* percent of all human cancers.
The tumour suppressor gene *p53/TP53* is called the guardian of the genome; when DNA damage is irreparable, it triggers *apoptosis/programmed cell death* to eliminate the damaged cell.
HIV is a *retrovirus/Retrovirus* that uses the enzyme *reverse transcriptase/RT* to convert its RNA genome into DNA, which then integrates into the host genome using *integrase/Integrase*.
Chronic alcohol consumption leads to liver damage because ethanol is oxidised to *acetaldehyde/Acetaldehyde* by alcohol dehydrogenase, increasing the *NADH/NADH:NAD+* ratio and causing fatty liver.

overallFeedback.feedback: Review the material and try again.
Good effort! Review the areas you missed.
Excellent! You have a strong grasp of this topic.
